summary: High‑energy set with stellar Touch > Rain and a soaring second‑set suite
blurb: The show bursts out of the gate with a punchy "Touch of Grey" and tight jams on "Looks Like Rain". A highlight second‑set runs from "Dear Mr. Fantasy" through "The Other One" into an inspired "Wharf Rat", capped by a soaring encore "Gloria".
review: April 14, 1985 at Irvine Meadows finds the Dead firing on all cylinders. The first set is a rapid‑fire romp – Jerry’s lead work on “Touch of Grey” and “Looks Like Rain” is described as flawless and crisp, while Brent soars with lush chords that lift the whole band. Bob’s vocal presence shines especially on “Sugaree”, and Phil lays down wavy bass foundations during the expansive “Space”. The second set builds a gentle groove into one of the night’s most memorable sequences: "Dear Mr. Fantasy → The Other One → Wharf Rat" flows seamlessly, showcasing the group’s tight interplay and improvisational depth. A calm yet powerful encore with “Gloria” rounds out an evening that balances high‑octane energy with relaxed, melodic exploration. Across multiple recordings the performance is uniformly praised, making this a must‑listen for fans of the mid‑80s era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings label the show as positive, repeatedly highlighting standout jams (Touch of Grey, Looks Like Rain, Dear Mr. Fantasy → The Other One → Wharf Rat) and praising Jerry’s lead work, Brent’s keyboards, and overall band tightness. No negative sentiment appears across analyses, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard mix that captures the high‑energy performance and highlights like Looks Like Rain, Touch of Grey, and Sugaree with minimal technical flaws.

key_highlights: Punchy opening with "Touch of Grey" setting a high‑energy tone.
"Looks Like Rain" showcases Brent’s soaring keyboards and Jerry’s crisp lead.
Second‑set suite "Dear Mr. Fantasy → The Other One → Wharf Rat" flows seamlessly and is praised as a highlight.
Encore "Gloria" provides a soaring, memorable close.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work is repeatedly called flawless, crisp and even "catastrophic" in a good way, topping jams like Touch of Grey and Looks Like Rain.
band_performance.Phil: Phil supplies wavy bass underpinnings during Space and anchors the high‑energy set with solid groove.
band_performance.Bob: Weir delivers strong vocal cues on Sugaree and provides reliable rhythm throughout the show.
band_performance.Brent: Brent earns special praise for soaring keyboard passages on Looks Like Rain, adding a bright texture to the mix.
